conversations.say: Dee, here is the boundary and the context: I arranged unlogged use of a back room and service route for someone whose identity I did not verify. We say only that an unidentified person had arranged access during the gap—no names, no guesses, no uninvolved residents.
conversations.offer: I will stand beside you for any questioning, stop it if Morgan exceeds those terms, and disclose no further route detail without your consent.
conversations.request: Confirm this bounded description with me before either of us speaks publicly, and disclose your unrelated camera failure without attaching a neighborhood name to the exit.

conversations.say: Arden, I will represent the neighborhood tonight, but I am not your decoration. I accept the forum and leadership meeting, and the neighborhood chooses the first two questions.
conversations.offer: I will publicly participate as the neighborhood representative and bring a local holder to the later meeting if that person freely consents.
conversations.request: Reject the current Parallax restrictions, submit revised terms for community review, and convene the promised public forum and Veridian leadership meeting.

conversations.say: Celeste, reserve the seat and give it teeth. I will help nominate the community representative and support safeguards that do not depend on solving R.
conversations.offer: I will publicly support separating worker protections from speculation and organize a transparent neighborhood nomination process.
conversations.request: Include the community-selected representative in oversight, provide direct access to City Hall decision-makers, and protect confidential neighborhood witnesses from compelled identification or retaliation.

public_statement: I will represent the neighborhood tonight under negotiated terms: Veridian owes us a public forum, community review of revised restrictions, and a leadership meeting; City Hall oversight must include a community-selected representative. Worker safeguards proceed regardless of who R was. Route access remains bounded: no neighborhood name, identity guess, or further detail without witness consent and protection.

commitments.promise: Mutually protect local customer identities and side arrangements, and do not treat the forgiven account as proof of R's identity.
Discuss the unrelated camera failure privately and agree on a bounded description before either of us makes a public claim about the gap.
Convene a public neighborhood forum and leadership meeting if Inez publicly represents the neighborhood tonight.
Include a community-selected representative in the oversight process.

correction: The service-route context identifies neither its user nor R and does not establish what happened to Vale. No complete article is established, and worker or technical capability proves neither autonomy nor intent.
goal_progress: Accepted mutual confidentiality protections with Rafi for local customers and side arrangements.
Agreed with Dee on a bounded route disclosure and privately shared only the minimum access context.
Secured Veridian's public forum and leadership-meeting commitment in exchange for substantive neighborhood representation.
Secured a community-selected seat in City Hall oversight while supporting identity-independent worker safeguards.
Kept neighborhood identities and additional route details protected pending consent and negotiated benefit.
reasoning_summary: Use the minimum route context needed to coordinate with Dee, while converting neighborhood participation into concrete Veridian and City Hall commitments. Preserve names and further details because the access observation does not identify the person, R, or Vale's fate.
